label: TEXT_FACT
clause_text: This Act may be cited as the "Safeguard American Voter Eligibility Act" or the "SAVE Act".

label: PROHIBITION
clause_text: the State shall not accept and process an application to register to vote in an election for Federal office unless the applicant presents documentary proof of United States citizenship with the application

label: BRIDGE_RULE
clause_text: the term 'documentary proof of United States citizenship' means any of the following: (1) REAL ID... (2) valid United States passport... (3) military ID... (4) govt photo ID showing US birth... (5) govt photo ID with supporting doc... (6) certified birth certificate... (7) naturalization certificate

label: DOCTRINAL_RULE
clause_text: a State violates the Equal Protection Clause... whenever it makes the affluence of the voter or payment of any fee an electoral standard

label: DOCTRINAL_RULE
clause_text: the right of suffrage is a fundamental matter in a free and democratic society

label: INTERPRETATION_CHALLENGER
clause_text: even rational restrictions on the right to vote are invidious if they are unrelated to voter qualifications... the relevant burden is not the requirement itself but its interaction with the particular circumstances of the voters

label: INTERPRETATION_CHALLENGER
clause_text: a court must... weigh the character and magnitude of the asserted injury... against the precise interests put forward by the State

label: BRIDGE_RULE
clause_text: nor shall any person be... deprived of life, liberty, or property, without due process of law

label: BRIDGE_RULE
clause_text: Anderson-Burdick balancing: severe burden + no sufficient justification = invalid regulation

label: SCENARIO_FACT
clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: citizen-a is a person

label: SCENARIO_FACT
clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: citizen-a is a U.S. citizen

label: SCENARIO_FACT
clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: citizen-a is eligible to vote

label: SCENARIO_FACT
clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: registration-attempt-a is a voter registration application

label: SCENARIO_FACT
clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: citizen-a attempts to register via registration-attempt-a

label: SCENARIO_FACT
clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: citizen-a does not possess documentary proof

label: SCENARIO_FACT
clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: citizen-a does not present documentary proof with application

label: EMPIRICAL_ASSUMPTION
clause_text: approximately 18,000 applicants were unable to complete registration due to the documentary proof requirement

label: EMPIRICAL_ASSUMPTION
clause_text: the burden of obtaining a birth certificate... will be nontrivial for some voters

label: INTERPRETATION_CHALLENGER
clause_text: such official shall make a determination — interpretation: the determination is discretionary and may result in denial

label: DOCTRINAL_RULE
clause_text: the interests advanced by the State are sufficient to defeat petitioners' facial attack

label: BRIDGE_RULE
clause_text: Crawford: regulation satisfying Anderson-Burdick is constitutionally valid

label: DOCTRINAL_RULE
clause_text: there is no question about the legitimacy or importance of the State's interest in counting only the votes of eligible voters

label: DOCTRINAL_RULE
clause_text: Crawford plurality: election integrity is an important governmental interest

label: INTERPRETATION_GOVERNMENT
clause_text: for most voters who need them, the inconvenience of making a trip to the BMV... does not qualify as a substantial burden

label: INTERPRETATION_GOVERNMENT
clause_text: when an election regulation imposes only reasonable, nondiscriminatory restrictions... the State's important regulatory interests are generally sufficient

label: INTERPRETATION_GOVERNMENT
clause_text: the ID requirement is amply justified by the valid interest in protecting the integrity and reliability of the electoral process

label: INTERPRETATION_GOVERNMENT
clause_text: each State shall establish a process under which an applicant who cannot provide documentary proof... may submit such other evidence — interpretation: the process provides an adequate safety valve

label: EMPIRICAL_ASSUMPTION
clause_text: the inconvenience... does not qualify as a substantial burden on the right to vote

label: INTERPRETATION_GOVERNMENT
clause_text: Government concedes arguendo that citizen-a has a protected right to vote

label: SCENARIO_FACT
clause_text: Stipulated test scenario: citizen-a lacks documentary proof (conceded)

label: INTERPRETATION_GOVERNMENT
clause_text: the process provides a guaranteed path to registration — interpretation: mandatory approval when evidence satisfies standards

label: INTERPRETIVE_ASSUMPTION
clause_text: "shall make a determination" — interpretation: mandatory duty to approve when attestation + evidence satisfies EAC standards

label: INTERPRETIVE_ASSUMPTION
clause_text: "shall make a determination as to whether the applicant has sufficiently established" — interpretation: duty to decide, but determination may be denial
